source: User clarification on 2026-05-12: cold data should be hidden as operating-system mechanics, not only UI filtering; UI user naming is CodeWiki, not Control Room.
summary: Accepted direction: CodeWiki should behave as a hot working-set operating context by default. Cold/archive data must be hidden from default state, graph, agency, and UI context unless the user explicitly asks to restore or audit archives. User-facing UI naming should be CodeWiki; Control Room may remain an internal implementation name.

diff_table.current_state: Default graph/state views expose some cold archive refs and restore index data alongside hot working-set data.
diff_table.desired_state: Default CodeWiki operating context exposes only hot working-set data; cold refs, closed-task details, old pass validations, and restore indexes are hidden by default.
diff_table.rationale: Agents should spend attention and tokens on active software-development state, not historical archive data.

diff_table.current_state: Cold data can be compact but still visible in default graph/UI context.
diff_table.desired_state: Cold data is available only through explicit user-requested restore/archive/audit surfaces.
diff_table.rationale: Archive access should be an intentional context expansion, not default operating memory.

diff_table.current_state: The local web UI and docs may use Control Room as a user-facing name.
diff_table.desired_state: Control Room may remain an internal code/module name, but user-facing UI labels should say CodeWiki.
diff_table.rationale: Consistent product naming reduces user confusion without requiring internal code churn.

accepted_decisions.summary: Cold/archive data requires explicit restore, archive, or audit request.
User-facing UI name is CodeWiki; Control Room is internal only.

assumptions: Initial implementation can filter default graph/state/UI surfaces without deleting archive metadata from canonical builds or generated graph internals.
Explicit archive/restore command implementation can remain metadata-gated if not yet present.
non_goals: Do not delete Git archive refs or implementation-build publication metadata.
Do not hide fail/block validation or active publication blockers.
Do not rename internal source files or classes solely for naming aesthetics.

knowledge: Graph/state/UI docs must specify hot-only default and explicit archive access.
roadmap: Create one task for hot-only default context and CodeWiki user-facing naming.
code: Filter default graph/state/UI views; preserve explicit archive access path.

knowledge: Graph/state/UI docs must specify hot-only default and explicit archive access.
